codeReview.summary: Reviewed the feature handoff, transcript skeleton, and commit d9c5435. The dashboard now labels export scope as filtered loaded matches, disables export when no filtered rows remain, deduplicates mixed stored/API history before rendering and export, and adds focused helper tests covering row mapping, CSV escaping, filename generation, and duplicate suppression. I did not find any blocking or non-blocking implementation defects against VAL-HISTORY-004 or VAL-CROSS-003.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The web-feature-worker skill should document a Windows-safe port-3005 cleanup fallback that avoids PowerShell $-variable interpolation, because the worker followed mission guidance but still hit an exec-wrapper quoting failure when trying to stop the dev server.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript skeleton for workerSessionId 9eef7b32-716c-41e6-8111-9dc4f49a39bc shows the manifest-style stop command failing with 5 was unexpected at this time. after a prior PowerShell-based cleanup attempt, and the handoff.skillFeedback suggests documenting a safer fallback.

summary: Pass. The history export implementation satisfies the documented filtered loaded-history scope, keeps export disabled for zero-result states, and preserves consistent filtering/export behavior after mixed stored-history and older-history loads without duplicate or dropped rows.
